name: AUSTIN HILL BARNETT
motto: "Lord, Lord, how this world is given to lying."

name: J. H. BRADBERRY
motto: "Be wise with speed, A fool at forty is a fool indeed."

name: JESSE E. BRANNEN
motto: "A youth to fame and fortune unknown."

motto: “I never felt the kiss of love, nor maiden’s hand in mine.”

name: H. L. Covington, Jr.

Corporal, Sergeant, and Captain Corps Cadets

name: W. R. Clements
motto: “A head to be let, unfurnished.”

name: William L. Erwin
motto: "Sentimentally I am disposed to harmony, But organically I am incapable of a tune."

name: Charles N. Feidelson
motto: "Truly genius is a species of madness."

Third Corporal, Second Sergeant, First Lieutenant, Co. A

name: Tomlinson Fort
motto: "This diabolical conglomeration of inconsistencies was brought about by the juxtaposition of innumerable contingencies"

name: GEORGE HAINS, JR.
motto: "Greater men than I may have lived, but I do not believe it."

name: H. GINSBERG
motto: "There is nothing ill that can dwell in such a temple."

name: J. GLENN GILES
motto: "He hath a loud laugh that bespeaks a vacant mind."
clubs: Winner Freshman Debaters Medal for Demosthenian

name: Jesse J. Hill
motto: "Who tied thee to that sword?"

name: D. B. Hodge
motto: "The elements were so mixed in him that nature might stand up before all the world and say this was a man."

name: J. Doyle Jones
motto: "Smooth runs the water where the brook is deep."

name: I. Clarence Levy
motto: "And still they gaze and still their wonder grew, That one small head could hold all that he knew."
clubs: First Sergeant and Captain Co. D

motto: "The man that hath a tongue I say is no man, if with that tongue he cannot win a woman."
clubs: Secretary, Vice-president and President Phi Kappa

name: Byron S. Keith
motto: "A hungry lean faced 'man'—a mere anatomy."

name: Moon, A. H.
motto: "Here will be an abusing of God's patience and the Queen's English."

name: McDougald, Walter E.
motto: "Eternal sunshine settles on his head."

name: Pope, Hollis T.
motto: "The Pope he leads a jolly life, As merry as the day is long."

name: Thomas Gray Stokes
motto: "How much in love with himself and that without a rival."

name: Alexander W. Smith, Jr.
motto: "'Tis better to have loved and lost than never to have loved at all."
clubs: Second Sergeant, First Lieutenant Cadet Corps

name: James Jackson Ragan
motto: "My only books were woman's looks, and folly's all they taught me."

Corporal, Sergeant and First Lieutenant Cadet Corps

name: J. Roe Turner
motto: "Night after night he sat and bleared his eyes with books."

motto: "Much study is a weariness of the flesh."

motto: "His mother's pride, his father's joy."
